source_paper_title: New Combinatorial Insights for Monotone Apportionment
context:
Section 6 (Discussion), page 23

Whether a similar result holds without violating the house size is open.
title: Existence of a quota-compliant, population-monotone, ex-ante proportional randomized apportionment method with fixed house size
latex:
Given integers \(n\ge 2\), a population vector \(p=(p_1,\dots,p_n)\in \mathbb{N}^n\), and a house size \(H\in \mathbb{N}\), let \(P=\sum_{i=1}^n p_i\) and define the quota of state \(i\) as \(q_i=\frac{p_i}{P}H\). An apportionment outcome is a (possibly randomized) vector \(X=(X_1,\dots,X_n)\in \mathbb{N}_0^n\) such that \(\sum_{i=1}^n X_i=H\) almost surely.

The problem is to determine whether there exists a randomized apportionment method \(\mathcal{M}\) that, for every \((p,H)\), outputs such a random vector \(X=\mathcal{M}(p,H)\) satisfying all of the following properties:

- (Ex-post quota compliance) For every state \(i\in[n]\), \(X_i\in\{\lfloor q_i\rfloor,\lceil q_i\rceil\}\) almost surely.
- (Population monotonicity) For any two instances \((p,H)\) and \((p',H')\), for any states \(i,j\in[n]\), if \(\frac{p'_i}{p'_j}\ge \frac{p_i}{p_j}\), then it does not occur with positive probability that simultaneously \(\mathcal{M}_i(p,H) > \mathcal{M}_i(p',H')\) and \(\mathcal{M}_j(p,H) < \mathcal{M}_j(p',H')\). Equivalently, population monotonicity holds almost surely over the method’s internal randomness.
- (Ex-ante proportionality) For every state \(i\in[n]\), \(\mathbb{E}[X_i]=q_i\).

In particular, \(\mathcal{M}\) must satisfy the fixed-house-size constraint \(\sum_i X_i=H\) with probability \(1\), i.e., it may not “violate the house size” ex post.
open_problem_quote: Whether a similar result holds without violating the house size is open.

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
